text.title: Agent Reasoning Depth Budget Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example applying agent-reasoning-depth-budget to a representative case. Walks step-by-step through the procedure, shows the produced artefact, and highlights the rules it satisfies.

section.title: Setup
section.p: Context: a team that matches the Applies-If conditions for agent-reasoning-depth-budget. Inputs are populated per the Prerequisites table.

list.item: Step 1: Pick 4 budgets to sweep (e.g., 1024 / 2048 / 4096 / 8192 tokens). — produced sweep plan.
Step 2: Run eval at each budget; record score and cost. — produced budget table.
Step 3: Apply r2 Pareto pick. — produced budget decision.
Step 4: Add under/over detectors per r3. — produced detectors live.
Step 5: Implement inter-turn compression per r4. — produced compression live.
Step 6: Document budget table; schedule re-eval at next model release. — produced budget-table.md.

section.title: Final artefact
section.p: The team commits the final artefact (per spec shape) at the version-controlled path declared in the output contract. Owner signs and links it to downstream consumers.

list.item: r1: Start with the smallest budget the provider allows (or 1024 tokens); double until eval-score gain ≤2% per doubling; stop
r2: Record budget × score × cost for at least 4 budgets; pick the smallest budget within 2% of the best score (Pareto pick).
r3: Under-thinking detector: ≥30% of failures come from reasoning that stopped before a known sub-step. Over-thinking: judge
r4: Inter-turn compression: at turn N>2, prior thinking traces are summarized to ≤256 tokens before being re-included.
r5: Re-evaluate budget after every model version upgrade; old budgets may over-pay or under-think on the new model.
